_comment: Verified SuiteQL query library. Every entry was executed successfully against a real NetSuite account (used only for testing) and targets REAL NetSuite analytics tables — not the demo's simplified schema. The generation layer retrieves and adapts these before writing SQL from scratch. `sql` uses :param placeholders the model substitutes. No account-specific data is stored here; sample_shape shows column names only.
verified_dialect: NetSuite SuiteQL (Oracle-flavoured). Supports TO_DATE, TO_CHAR, TRUNC, ADD_MONTHS, SYSDATE, NVL, FETCH FIRST n ROWS ONLY, BUILTIN.DF. OFFSET is unreliable — page with rowBegin/rowEnd instead.

queries.intent: Total or period revenue from the general ledger (canonical, avoids line-sign and tax traps)
queries.sql:
SELECT ap.periodname AS period, SUM(tal.credit - tal.debit) AS revenue
FROM transactionaccountingline tal
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tal.transaction
JOIN account a ON a.id = tal.account
JOIN accountingperiod ap ON ap.id = t.postingperiod
WHERE tal.posting = 'T'
  AND a.accttype = 'Income'
  AND ap.isadjust = 'F'
  AND ap.startdate BETWEEN TO_DATE(:start, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND TO_DATE(:end, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
GROUP BY ap.periodname, ap.startdate
ORDER BY ap.startdate
queries.caveats: Revenue is credit-signed, so credit - debit is positive. posting='T' excludes non-posting orders. isadjust='F' drops period-rollup rows. This is the most robust 'revenue' definition; use REV-002 only when the user explicitly wants item/line detail.

queries.intent: Revenue broken down by item / product, at the line level
queries.sql:
SELECT i.itemid, i.displayname,
       SUM(-tl.netamount) AS revenue,
       SUM(-tl.quantity) AS units
FROM transactionline tl
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tl.transaction
JOIN item i ON i.id = tl.item
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND tl.mainline = 'F'
  AND tl.taxline = 'F'
  AND i.itemtype IN ('InvtPart', 'NonInvtPart', 'Assembly', 'Service', 'Kit')
  AND t.trandate BETWEEN TO_DATE(:start, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND TO_DATE(:end, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
GROUP BY i.itemid, i.displayname
ORDER BY revenue DESC
FETCH FIRST :n ROWS ONLY
queries.caveats: CRITICAL: sales item lines have NEGATIVE netamount and quantity, so negate them (-tl.netamount). mainline='F' drops the header total row; taxline='F' drops tax lines; the itemtype filter drops Subtotal/Markup/Discount pseudo-lines. Without all of these the numbers are wrong and 'best selling' sorts backwards.

queries.intent: Simple invoiced total from transaction headers (includes tax; fast)
queries.sql:
SELECT SUM(t.foreigntotal) AS invoiced_total
FROM transaction t
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND t.trandate BETWEEN TO_DATE(:start, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND TO_DATE(:end, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
queries.caveats: foreigntotal is the header total in the transaction's own currency and is tax-INCLUSIVE. Use for 'total invoiced'; use REV-001 for true (tax-exclusive, GL) revenue. Do not multiply by exchangerate unless consolidating (see CUR-001).

queries.intent: Monthly revenue trend from the GL
queries.sql:
SELECT TO_CHAR(t.trandate, 'YYYY-MM') AS month, SUM(tal.credit - tal.debit) AS revenue
FROM transactionaccountingline tal
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tal.transaction
JOIN account a ON a.id = tal.account
WHERE tal.posting = 'T'
  AND a.accttype = 'Income'
  AND t.trandate >= ADD_MONTHS(TRUNC(SYSDATE, 'MM'), -12)
GROUP BY TO_CHAR(t.trandate, 'YYYY-MM')
ORDER BY month
queries.caveats: Groups by transaction date. For posting-period grouping join accountingperiod on t.postingperiod instead (see PERIOD-001).

queries.intent: Open accounts-receivable balance by customer (who owes us)
queries.sql:
SELECT c.companyname, SUM(t.foreignamountunpaid) AS open_ar
FROM transaction t
JOIN customer c ON c.id = t.entity
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND t.foreignamountunpaid > 0
GROUP BY c.companyname
HAVING SUM(t.foreignamountunpaid) > :threshold
ORDER BY open_ar DESC
queries.caveats: CRITICAL: open balance = foreignamountunpaid, NOT foreigntotal. 'Open' invoices are those with foreignamountunpaid > 0 — do NOT filter status = 'open' (status is a raw code, not that word). entity joins customer for AR types.

queries.sql:
SELECT CASE
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) THEN 'current'
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) - 30 THEN '1-30'
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) - 60 THEN '31-60'
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) - 90 THEN '61-90'
         ELSE '90+'
       END AS bucket,
       COUNT(*) AS invoices,
       SUM(t.foreignamountunpaid) AS amount
FROM transaction t
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND t.foreignamountunpaid > 0
GROUP BY CASE
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) THEN 'current'
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) - 30 THEN '1-30'
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) - 60 THEN '31-60'
         WHEN t.duedate >= TRUNC(SYSDATE) - 90 THEN '61-90'
         ELSE '90+'
       END
queries.caveats: Aging is measured from duedate against SYSDATE. The CASE must be repeated verbatim in GROUP BY (SuiteQL has no GROUP BY alias). Uses foreignamountunpaid for the outstanding amount.

queries.intent: Account balance for a given accounting period
queries.sql:
SELECT a.acctnumber, a.fullname, SUM(tal.amount) AS balance
FROM transactionaccountingline tal
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tal.transaction
JOIN account a ON a.id = tal.account
JOIN accountingperiod ap ON ap.id = t.postingperiod
WHERE tal.posting = 'T'
  AND ap.periodname = :period
  AND ap.isadjust = 'F'
  AND a.acctnumber = :acctnumber
GROUP BY a.acctnumber, a.fullname
queries.caveats: tal.amount is the signed GL amount (debit positive, credit negative). Join accountingperiod on t.postingperiod, not trandate. isadjust='F' avoids double counting adjustment rows.

queries.intent: Profit & loss (income, expenses, net) by month
queries.sql:
SELECT TO_CHAR(t.trandate, 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
       SUM(CASE WHEN a.accttype = 'Income' THEN tal.credit - tal.debit ELSE 0 END) AS income,
       SUM(CASE WHEN a.accttype IN ('COGS', 'Expense', 'OthExpense') THEN tal.debit - tal.credit ELSE 0 END) AS expenses,
       SUM(CASE WHEN a.accttype = 'Income' THEN tal.credit - tal.debit
                WHEN a.accttype IN ('COGS', 'Expense', 'OthExpense') THEN -(tal.debit - tal.credit)
                ELSE 0 END) AS net
FROM transactionaccountingline tal
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tal.transaction
JOIN account a ON a.id = tal.account
WHERE tal.posting = 'T'
  AND t.trandate BETWEEN TO_DATE(:start, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND TO_DATE(:end, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
GROUP BY TO_CHAR(t.trandate, 'YYYY-MM')
ORDER BY month
queries.caveats: Income accounts are credit-positive (credit-debit); expense accounts are debit-positive (debit-credit). posting='T' only.

queries.sql:
SELECT v.companyname, SUM(-t.foreigntotal) AS spend
FROM transaction t
JOIN vendor v ON v.id = t.entity
WHERE t.type = 'VendBill'
  AND t.trandate BETWEEN TO_DATE(:start, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND TO_DATE(:end, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
GROUP BY v.companyname
ORDER BY spend DESC
FETCH FIRST :n ROWS ONLY
queries.caveats: Vendor-bill foreigntotal is credit-signed (negative), so negate it for a positive spend figure. entity joins vendor for AP types.

queries.intent: Open vendor bills (what we still owe suppliers)
queries.sql:
SELECT v.companyname, t.tranid, t.trandate, t.duedate, ABS(t.foreignamountunpaid) AS unpaid
FROM transaction t
JOIN vendor v ON v.id = t.entity
WHERE t.type = 'VendBill'
  AND t.foreignamountunpaid <> 0
ORDER BY t.duedate
queries.caveats: Bill unpaid amounts are credit-signed; ABS() makes them positive. Filter foreignamountunpaid <> 0 for still-open bills.

queries.sql:
SELECT BUILTIN.DF(tl.subsidiary) AS subsidiary, SUM(-tl.netamount) AS revenue
FROM transactionline tl
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tl.transaction
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND tl.mainline = 'F'
  AND tl.taxline = 'F'
GROUP BY BUILTIN.DF(tl.subsidiary)
ORDER BY revenue DESC
queries.caveats: CRITICAL: transaction.subsidiary is NOT_EXPOSED for SuiteQL and errors. Use transactionline.subsidiary instead. BUILTIN.DF() turns the internal id into the subsidiary name.

queries.intent: Revenue by department or class (line-level dimensions)
queries.sql:
SELECT BUILTIN.DF(tl.department) AS department, SUM(-tl.netamount) AS revenue
FROM transactionline tl
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tl.transaction
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND tl.mainline = 'F'
  AND tl.taxline = 'F'
  AND t.trandate BETWEEN TO_DATE(:start, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AND TO_DATE(:end, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
GROUP BY BUILTIN.DF(tl.department)
ORDER BY revenue DESC
queries.caveats: Department/class/location live on the LINE (transactionline), not the header. Filter mainline='F' AND taxline='F' and negate netamount. Swap tl.department for tl.class or tl.location as needed.

queries.intent: Revenue consolidated to base currency across subsidiaries
queries.sql:
SELECT BUILTIN.DF(tl.subsidiary) AS subsidiary,
       SUM(-tl.netamount) AS revenue_txn_currency,
       SUM(-tl.netamount * t.exchangerate) AS revenue_base
FROM transactionline tl
JOIN transaction t ON t.id = tl.transaction
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND tl.mainline = 'F'
  AND tl.taxline = 'F'
GROUP BY BUILTIN.DF(tl.subsidiary)
ORDER BY revenue_base DESC
queries.caveats: t.exchangerate converts the transaction's own currency to ITS subsidiary's base currency — it is NOT a single global rate. Summing base amounts across subsidiaries with different base currencies is only meaningful if they share one; otherwise report per-subsidiary and state the assumption. For true consolidated reporting NetSuite uses consolidatedexchangerate.

queries.intent: Available stock on hand per item and location
queries.sql:
SELECT BUILTIN.DF(inv.item) AS item, BUILTIN.DF(inv.location) AS location,
       SUM(inv.quantityonhand) AS on_hand,
       SUM(inv.quantityavailable) AS available
FROM inventorybalance inv
GROUP BY BUILTIN.DF(inv.item), BUILTIN.DF(inv.location)
HAVING SUM(inv.quantityavailable) < :threshold
ORDER BY available
queries.caveats: inventorybalance holds quantityonhand and quantityavailable per item/location. BUILTIN.DF resolves item/location ids to names without extra joins. Drop the HAVING for a full stock list.

queries.intent: Filter or display transaction status correctly
queries.sql:
SELECT t.tranid, BUILTIN.DF(t.status) AS status_label, t.foreignamountunpaid
FROM transaction t
WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc'
  AND t.foreignamountunpaid > 0
ORDER BY t.trandate DESC
FETCH FIRST :n ROWS ONLY
queries.caveats: transaction.status stores a raw per-type code (e.g. CustInvc 'A'=Open, 'B'=Paid In Full) — never filter status = 'open'. Use BUILTIN.DF(t.status) to DISPLAY the label, and business fields (foreignamountunpaid > 0 for open) to FILTER.
